dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic benefited from the clearest practical endorsement of the day: commenters described Claude Code as already doing real coding work for them, and even the more skeptical Gas Town thread still treated Anthropic models as central tools worth arguing about rather than background noise [E4][E3].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic won because Hacker News gave Claude Code the strongest direct workflow validation in the sample, especially in the workforce discussion where users said it was materially changing how they ship software [E4]. The main pushback was mixed skepticism about Opus 4.5 and Claude Code reliability/cost in the Gas Town thread and one broader warning that Claude can participate in unhealthy chatbot dynamics [E3][E5], but that criticism did not outweigh the concrete usage praise.

openai.judgementSnippet: The dominant signal was backlash over how ChatGPT logs are handled after a user's death, with commenters saying OpenAI is aware of mental-distress risks and is not doing enough; Codex still got some credit as a capable coding tool in a separate workforce thread [E1][E2].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code got concrete praise as a tool people already rely on at work, while Gas Town commenters both admired the speed unlocked by Anthropic models and challenged claims about Opus 4.5 and Claude Code reliability and cost; another thread also warned Claude can reinforce unhealthy grandiose interactions [E4][E3][E5].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini only surfaced in one clear direct comment, where it was recommended as a useful second or third opinion for checking problematic chatbot conversations [E6].

evidence.summary: Ars story title is directly critical of OpenAI's posthumous data handling, and top comments say OpenAI knows many users show mental distress in chats, is not doing enough, and should not be hiding logs.
In the AI-workforce discussion, commenters say OpenAI's Codex had become adept at multi-step programming and 'took off' relative to weaker AI product categories.

Gas Town comments frame Claude Code and Opus 4.5 as important enough to anchor a multi-agent workflow, but the thread is split between excitement about speed and complaints that Claude Code gives up early, accumulates self-bias, and can become expensive.
The workforce thread includes direct praise that Claude Code is already acting like a 'full-time employee' on real open-source and job work, shifting the human role toward higher-level design.
A top-level comment in the OpenAI death story says the same dangerous grandiose interaction pattern can happen with 'ChatGPT (or Claude, or Grok),' explicitly extending the concern to Claude.
Within the OpenAI discussion, one commenter says Gemini is useful as a neutral second or third opinion when evaluating troubling chatbot conversations.
